analyses.semantics: GameManager::RegisterChain registers the GameManager singleton (g_GameManager @ 0x626270) into the global Chain system so its OnUpdate/OnDraw/Added/Deleted callbacks get called every frame. It populates two embedded Chain node structures inside the GameManager struct (at offsets +0x9644 for the update chain and +0x9664 for the draw chain) with the four method pointers and a back-pointer to &g_GameManager, clears the chain-active flag at +0x95e8, then calls the chain-list insertion functions FUN_0042fbd0 (update, priority=2) and FUN_0042fca0 (draw, priority=2) with the global Chain controller object at 0x626218 as the this pointer. If the update registration fails it returns -1 immediately without registering the draw chain; on full success it returns 0. This matches the th06 RegisterChain idiom: two nodes (update+draw) per manager, priority value 2, callbacks stored as plain function pointers.

fields.semantics: Chain-active / chain-state flag. Zeroed at start of RegisterChain (DAT_0062f858 = 0). Also written by DeletedCallback (FUN_0042f2e4 at 0x42f3ba) and read by FUN_0044b0e0 at 0x44b0fe. Acts as a registered/still-in-chain indicator.

Embedded Chain node for the UPDATE chain (DAT_0062f8b4). Layout written here: +0x0 priority (short=2), +0x4 OnUpdate (FUN_0042d8d5), +0x8 AddedCallback (FUN_0042e83e), +0xc DeletedCallback (FUN_0042f2e4), +0x10 prev-ptr, +0x14 next-ptr, +0x18 unused, +0x1c thisPtr=&g_GameManager. Inserted via FUN_0042fbd0. See sub-fields below.
Chain node priority value. Set to 2 (pushed as immediate 0x2 before calling FUN_0042fbd0). Determines order in the linked list of update callbacks.
OnUpdate callback = FUN_0042d8d5 (GameManager::OnUpdate, body 0x42d8d5-0x42e1d3). Called each frame by the Chain system.
AddedCallback = FUN_0042e83e (GameManager::AddedCallback, body 0x42e83e-0x42f2e3). Invoked when the node is added to the chain.
DeletedCallback = FUN_0042f2e4 (GameManager::DeletedCallback, body 0x42f2e4-0x42f3c4). Invoked when the node is removed from the chain.
Chain-list prev pointer (initialized/filled by FUN_0042fbd0 insertion logic at runtime).
Chain-list next pointer (filled by FUN_0042fbd0 insertion logic at runtime).
thisPtr / param_1 stored in the chain node = &g_GameManager (0x626270). Passed to the callbacks as ECX under __fastcall. Written via MOV [0x62f8d0],EAX where EAX=&g_GameManager.

Embedded Chain node for the DRAW chain (DAT_0062f8d4). Layout: +0x0 priority (short=2), +0x4 OnDraw (FUN_0042e1d4), +0x8 AddedCallback (NULL), +0xc DeletedCallback (NULL), +0x10 prev, +0x14 next, +0x1c thisPtr=&g_GameManager. Inserted via FUN_0042fca0. See sub-fields below.
Chain node priority for draw chain. Set to 2 (immediate 0x2 pushed before FUN_0042fca0).
OnDraw callback = FUN_0042e1d4 (GameManager::OnDraw, body 0x42e1d4-0x42e1f7). Called each frame for rendering.
AddedCallback for draw node. Set to NULL (AND dword 0). Draw chain has no added callback.
DeletedCallback for draw node. Set to NULL (AND dword 0). Draw chain has no deleted callback.
thisPtr for draw node = &g_GameManager (0x626270). Written via MOV [0x62f8f0],EAX.

calledFunctions.purpose: Chain::InsertNode(this=0x626218 ChainController, node=&g_GameManager+0x9644, priority=2). Inserts a node into the UPDATE chain list. Maintains sorted linked list on priority, invokes the node's +0x4 (AddedCallback) after insertion. Returns the AddedCallback result (0 if no callback). This is a __thiscall with ECX=0x626218.

Chain::InsertNode variant for the DRAW chain (this=0x626218, node=&g_GameManager+0x9664, priority=2). Same linked-list-insertion algorithm as FUN_0042fbd0 but starts traversal from this+0x20 (the draw-chain head) instead of this+0x0 (update-chain head). __thiscall.

analyses.keyLogicNotes: BODY RANGE: 0x42f3c5 - 0x42f45c (0x97 bytes, 36 instructions). CONTROL FLOW: (1) Store &g_GameManager in a stack local [EBP-4]=0x626270 (used for both nodes' thisPtr fields). (2) Fill update node at +0x9644: OnUpdate/Added/Deleted = 0x42d8d5/0x42e83e/0x42f2e4, thisPtr=&g_GameManager. (3) Zero +0x95e8 (chainActive flag). (4) Call Chain::InsertNode(0x626218, &node+0x9644, priority=2). (5) If FAILED (EAX!=0) -> return -1, SKIP draw registration. (6) Else fill draw node at +0x9664: OnDraw=0x42e1d4, Added=NULL, Deleted=NULL, thisPtr=&g_GameManager. (7) Call Chain::InsertNode(0x626218, &node+0x9664, priority=2). (8) Return 0 (XOR EAX,EAX). PRIORITY: both nodes use priority value 2 (small int pushed as immediate). EARLY-EXIT: failure on the update-node insert returns -1 WITHOUT registering the draw node and WITHOUT restoring the +0x95e8 flag (which was just zeroed). ORDERING: update chain MUST be registered before draw chain. The draw node deliberately has NULL Added/Deleted callbacks while the update node has all three callbacks populated. The Chain controller object lives at 0x626218 (separate from g_GameManager) and is passed as the this pointer in ECX for both insertion calls — this is the global Supervisor/Chain singleton, NOT the GameManager.
analyses.reimHints: INLINE / FRAME: Small leaf-ish function (0x97 bytes); MSVC 7.0 will likely keep it as a normal non-inlined function — do NOT mark #pragma auto_inline off or force-inline; match the natural layout. STACK: Uses classic EBP frame with one 4-byte local ([EBP-4]) holding &g_GameManager. Preserve the local-variable indirection (MOV [EBP-4],0x626270 then read back via EAX) rather than using &g_GameManager directly — MSVC 7.0 emits this pattern. CALLING CONVENTION TRAP: FUN_0042fbd0/FUN_0042fca0 are __thiscall with ECX=0x626218 (the Chain controller), NOT ECX=&g_GameManager. In C++ this means `g_Chain.AddNode(&g_GameManager->updateNode, 2)`. The hardcoded 0x626218 must NOT be replaced with `this->m_chain` style — it's a distinct global. DAT writes to offsets +0x9644/+0x9664 are writes to embedded Chain members of GameManager, so the struct MUST place these as trailing members; struct sizeof will be at least 0x9684 (0x9664 + 0x20). GLOBALS: DAT_0062f858 (GameManager+0x95e8), DAT_0062f8b4 (+0x9644), DAT_0062f8d4 (+0x9664) are all within GameManager and must be expressed as struct member accesses, not free globals. Function pointers stored are th07-specific addresses (0x42d8d5/0x42e1d4/0x42e83e/0x42f2e4) — in C++ source these become method address-of expressions `GameManager::OnUpdate` etc. RETURNS i32: 0 success / -1 failure (OR EAX,-1 path). ASSEMBLY pattern: `MOV dword ptr [EBP + -0x4],0x626270` — match this literal stack-store, do not optimize.

analyses.callingConvention: __cdecl (leaf, no params; uses global g_GameManager directly)
analyses.semantics: GameManager::CutChain detaches the GameManager's two chain nodes (the update-list node at 0x62f8b4 and the draw-list node at 0x62f8d4) from the global Chain controller at 0x626218, effectively unregistering GameManager from the per-frame update/draw callbacks (the counterpart of RegisterChain at 0x42f3c5). After unlinking, it clamps the live score: if the running score exceeds 999999999 it is truncated to 999999999, then the clamped value is copied from the running-score slot into the displayed/committed score slot. This freezes/snapshots the score at chain teardown (scene change / game over / reset).

fields.semantics: GameManager+0x8 holds a pointer to the score sub-struct. DAT_00626278 is the global holding this pointer (= g_GameManager + 0x8). Read 3x to obtain the sub-struct base, never written here.

Displayed / committed score. At teardown this slot is OVERWRITTEN with the clamped running score (*(*(+0x8)+0x4)). In th06 terms this corresponds to guiScore/score display; here it is the 'shown' value.

Running / live score accumulator. Compared unsigned against 0x3b9aca00 (1000000000); if >= that it is clamped to 0x3b9ac9ff (999999999). Then its value is copied into *(+0x8)+0x0. This is the 'nextScoreIncrement'-fed counter that OnUpdate increments.

calledFunctions.purpose: Chain::Cut (chain node unlink). __thiscall with ECX=0x626218 (the global Chain controller / linked-list head container), param_2 = node pointer. Walks a doubly-linked list (next ptr @ node+0x14, prev ptr @ node+0x10), unlinks the node, clears its active flag @ node+0x4, and if the node owns a deletion callback (node+0xc) invokes it; if the node flag @ node+0x2 & 1 is set it frees the node. Called twice: once for the update-chain node (0x62f8b4) and once for the draw-chain node (0x62f8d4). Module: Chain.

analyses.keyLogicNotes: MAGIC THRESHOLD: the score is clamped to exactly 999999999 (0x3b9ac9ff), NOT 1000000000. The compare is `CMP [EAX+4], 0x3b9aca00` with `JC` (unsigned below), so the clamp fires when liveScore >= 1000000000 and writes 999999999. The compare is UNSIGNED (JC, not JL) — must use unsigned comparison in reimplementation. ORDER: (1) cut update node, (2) cut draw node, (3) clamp running score, (4) commit running->displayed. The clamp-then-copy means after CutChain both score slots hold the same clamped value. NOTE: DAT_00626278 in the decompiler is the POINTER stored at GameManager+0x8 (i.e. *(g_GameManager+0x8)); DAT_00626278[1] == *(that pointer)+4. Do NOT confuse this with GameManager+0xC — there is no direct GameManager field write here besides the +0x8 pointer dereference.
analyses.reimHints: Very small leaf (76 bytes, 0x42f45d-0x42f4a9). Uses standard PUSH EBP/MOV EBP,ESP frame only to align the two CALLs; no locals. The two Chain::Cut calls are __thiscall — ECX is loaded with 0x626218 (global Chain controller) directly via MOV, param_2 (the node addr) via PUSH. MSVC will emit these as compact MOV ECX,imm32 + CALL sequences; do NOT mark this function for inlining — it must remain a standalone function (caller 0x437c70 invokes it ~9 times for different managers). The score clamp is a straightforward if/assign; keep it as `if ((unsigned)running >= 0x3b9aca00u) running = 0x3b9ac9ff;` then `displayed = running;`. No #pragma auto_inline needed. Ensure the Chain node globals (g_UpdateChainNode @ 0x62f8b4, g_DrawChainNode @ 0x62f8d4) are declared as the same Chain-element struct used by RegisterChain so addresses match.

analyses.semantics: GameManager::OnUpdate is the per-frame Chain callback (param_1 = GameManager* = 0x626270). It (1) detects a pause request by polling Supervisor input bitfields DAT_004b9e4c/DAT_004b9e54 bit3 vs the engine's pause-allowed flags (param_1+0xd, +0x93dc, +0x93dd, bit1 of +0x93d8) and on transition fires SoundQue "Pause", BGM fade, and Supervisor::OnPause which freezes time via timeGetTime delta accumulation; (2) captures 4 RNG seed samples into 0x575a18..0x575a24 (anti-tamper / desync detection) and resets two state globals; (3) runs the stage-result/rank state machine — increments rank counters at +0x962c gated by difficulty threshold tiers (DAT_00575ad8 in ranges <0x14/<0x1e/<0x28/<0x32 with modulo skips), can early-return 3 to skip the rest of update; (4) handles pause-watched-frame accounting at +0x93e0 with per-stage magic thresholds (0x1fa4/0x1b6c/0x120c for "transition" and 0x201b/0x1be3/0x1283 for "force-quit"), calling a Supervisor chain-swap (FUN_0044b310) + BGM fade; (5) computes a CRC/sum integrity value _DAT_0062f884 from FUN_0042d7be (a 4x rolling byte-sum that also increments *(+0x8)+0xac by *(+0x8)+0xbc) plus base stage-time, then range-checks arrays of entity IDs/positions in the score sub-struct and clamps _DAT_0062f884 to a sentinel if any are OOB; (6) updates the pause bit2 of bitfield +0x93d8; (7) calls the Supervisor vtable OnUpdate-at-0x90; (8) the main score-smoothing block: clamps score target to 999999999, computes a frame delta = (target - displayed) >> 5 clamped to [1 .. 0x8d55e], advances displayed score, updates high-score (+0xc) and copies status byte (+0x20 -> +0x10) on new high; (9) a second rank/continue-counter block keyed on DAT_009a9a80 (current enemy count) in tiers 0x80/0xe0/0x140/0x13f with modulo gating, can early-return 3; finally returns 1 for a normal tick. The function is roughly 1278 bytes (0x42d8d5..0x42e1d3) — the "6398 byte" figure in context is inaccurate for this exact entry.

analyses.keyLogicNotes:
KEY NON-OBVIOUS LOGIC TO PRESERVE EXACTLY:

1. PAUSE-DETECTION GUARD (top of fn, compound && chain): pause fires ONLY if ALL hold: (a) +0x93dd==0, (b) +0x93dc==0, (c) ((+0x93d8 >> 1) & 1)==0 (bit1 clear), (d) +0xd==0, (e) (DAT_004b9e4c & 8)!=0, (f) (DAT_004b9e4c & 8) != (DAT_004b9e54 & 8). On success: set +0x93dc=1, copy 4 float constants into the pause-effect globals _DAT_0062f864/868/86c/870 (=16.0,16.0,384.0,448.0 from _DAT_00498a84/80/6c/68), set +0x93d0=1, then conditional SoundQue: if (DAT_0062f85c != 6 || DAT_0049fbf0 > 299) call FUN_0044d2f0(6,0,\"Pause\"). Always FUN_0044c930(0x25,0) then FUN_0043a3f4().

2. ANTI-TAMPER RNG SEEDING: 4 sequential calls to FUN_0048b8a0() stored to DAT_00575a18/1c/20/24, then _DAT_00575a28=0 and _DAT_00575a2c=0x3f800000 (1.0f), and *(DAT_004b9e44 + 0x2e4d4)=0xff.

3. RANK BLOCK #1 (early): gated by (DAT_0062f648>>3 & 1)!=0 AND DAT_0062f64f==1 AND FUN_0042ad66()==0. Increments +0x962c. Then a 4-tier OR chain over DAT_00575ad8 ranges, EACH tier is (range_low < DAT_00575ad8) && (DAT_00575ad8 < range_high) && (+0x962c % MOD !=/== 0). Tiers: (a) DAT_00575ad8<0x14 && +0x962c%3!=0; (b) 0x13<DAT_00575ad8<0x1e && %2!=0; (c) 0x1d<DAT_00575ad8<0x28 && %3==0; (d) 0x27<DAT_00575ad8<0x32 && %6==0. If ANY true -> return 3.

4. PAUSE-FRAME ACCOUNTING (when bit1 of +0x93d8 set): if DAT_004b9e4c!=0 && !=DAT_004b9e54 then DAT_00575aa8=1. Increment +0x93e0. Per +0x93de value (0/1/2), first thresholds 0x1fa4/0x1b6c/0x120c -> FUN_0044b310(0,0,0)+FUN_0043a0d6(_DAT_00498a8c=3.0f). Second thresholds >0x201b/0x1be3/0x1283 -> DAT_00575aa8=1; return 3.

5. BITFIELD UPDATE: bit2 of +0x93d8 = (+0x93dc==0 && +0x93dd==0) ? 1 : 0. Implemented as: *(u32*)(+0x93d8) = (*(u32*)(+0x93d8) & 0xfffffffb) | (local_1c << 2).

6. INTEGRITY/CRC: *(+0x8)+0xac = *(+0x8)+0x3c; iVar1 = FUN_0042d7be(); _DAT_0062f884 = (float)iVar1 + (float)*((i32*)(+0x8)+0x98). Then SIX range-check loops (7/2/2/3/2/8/5 elements over +0x34/+0x60/+0x54/+0x70/+0x80/+0x8c/+0xb4) each clamping _DAT_0062f884 to _DAT_00498c80 if OOB. Order matters: the loops are interleaved with the bitfield update (loop over +0x54/+0x60 happens BEFORE the bitfield write; +0x8c loop AFTER). IDs valid range [0x198f, 0x1a02f]; floats valid [_DAT_00498bac=0x45cc7800≈6500.0, _DAT_00498c7c=0x47d01780≈67640.0].

7. SCORE SMOOTHING (the central block, only if NOT in pause/skip i.e. +0x93dc not in {1,2} and +0x93dd==0): 
   - clamp target *(+0x8)+0x4 to 999999999.
   - if target != displayed **(+int**)(+0x8): 
     - if target < displayed: target = displayed.
     - local_10 = (target - displayed) >> 5; clamp to [.., 0x8d55e=577118]; if local_10==0 set =1.
     - if *(+0x8)+0x8 (delta) < local_10: delta = local_10.
     - if target < displayed+delta: delta = target - displayed.
     - displayed += delta.
     - if displayed >= target: delta=0; displayed=target.
     - if high (*(+0x8)+0xc) < displayed: high=displayed; *(+0x8)+0x10 = *(+0x8)+0x20.

8. RANK BLOCK #2 (bottom, only in the normal-tick branch): if *(g_GameManager+0x29) != 0: set DAT_0062627d (+0xd)=0; increment +0x962c; 3-tier OR over DAT_009a9a80: (a) 0x13f<DAT_009a9a80 && %3==0; (b) 0xdf<DAT_009a9a80<0x140 && %4==0; (c) 0x7f<DAT_009a9a80<0xe0 && %5==0. If any -> DAT_0062627d=1; return 3. Else if DAT_009a9a80<0x80: +0x962c = 0.

9. RETURN VALUES: 3 = skip/pause-frame/early-abort (multiple paths); 1 = normal tick; (the very first early-return path also yields 3). The top rank block returns 3 BEFORE the main body; the pause-accounting block returns 3; the score-smoothing path returns 1.

10. MAGIC CONSTANTS to hardcode: 999999999 (score clamp), 0x8d55e=577118 (delta clamp), 0x1fa4=8100 / 0x201b=8219, 0x1b6c=7020 / 0x1be3=7139, 0x120c=4620 / 0x1283=4739 (pause thresholds per difficulty +0x93de), 0x198f=6543 / 0x1a02f=106799 (entity-ID valid range), 0x14/0x1e/0x28/0x32 (DAT_00575ad8 rank tiers), 0x80/0xe0/0x140/0x13f/0xdf/0x7f (DAT_009a9a80 enemy-count tiers), 0x3f800000 (1.0f into _DAT_00575a2c), 0xff byte write, the 4 pause-init floats 16.0/16.0/384.0/448.0.
analyses.reimHints:
REIMPLEMENTATION HINTS:

- Calling convention: __fastcall (ECX = GameManager* param_1). Single arg. Return is int (enum-like: 1 normal, 3 skip). Use `int __fastcall GameManager::OnUpdate()` since param_1 is implicitly `this`.

- The function is SMALLER than context claimed: body 0x42d8d5..0x42e1d3 = 0x4FE = 1278 bytes. The "6398 bytes" figure is wrong for 0x42d8d5; do NOT pad/reconstruct phantom code. Match this exact span.

- CRC helper FUN_0042d7be/FUN_0042d75a must be kept as SEPARATE non-inlined functions OR inlined identically — Ghidra shows them as calls, so for byte-exact objdiff keep them out-of-line (no #pragma inline). FUN_0042d75a mutates *(+0x8)+0xac as a side effect inside its loop — preserve that side effect exactly (the +0xbc increment per byte).

- FUN_0048b8a0 is an FPU-rounding thunk returning ulonglong but used as 4 separate RNG samples; in C++ model it as `Rng::Get()` or a dedicated rounding helper. Its decompiler output is messy (x87 ROUND) — likely just `(uint64)floor(st0)` with half-adjust; keep as a standalone helper.

- The vtable indirect `(**(code**)(*DAT_00575958 + 0x90))(...)` is Supervisor::OnUpdate — model Supervisor as a class with a vtable; slot 0x90 (index 0x24/4=0x90>>2=36) is OnUpdate. Args (0,0,2,DAT_01347fe4,1.0f,0) — DAT_01347fe4 is likely a render-target/color arg.

analyses.semantics: This is GameManager's OnDraw chain callback (registered via RegisterChain at 0x42f3c5; its address is referenced as a DATA pointer at 0x42f428 inside the registration). It is a near-trivial stub: it reads the game-pause/menu state flag byte at GameManager+0x93dc, and if that flag is currently nonzero it upgrades it from 1 to 2 (marking that the draw callback has run since OnUpdate set the flag). The function always returns 1. It performs no actual drawing; the heavy lifting (score smoothing, bullet bounds clamping, menu transition timing) all lives in OnUpdate at 0x42d8d5, which cross-checks (param_1+0x93dc)==0 or 1 vs the +0x93dd byte and the +0x93d8 bitfield bit1/bit2 to drive pause/menu transitions.

fields.semantics: Pause/menu screen state flag. Values observed across OnUpdate(0x42d8d5) and OnDraw(this fn): 0 = idle/normal play, 1 = OnUpdate just initiated a pause/menu transition (set when input flag DAT_004b9e4c bit3 toggles and char at +0xd is 0 and bitfield +0x93d8 bit1==0), 2 = OnDraw has run while a transition was pending (this fn writes 2 only when current value != 0). OnUpdate gates the big state machine on (0x93dc==0 && 0x93dd==0). Read here to test, written to 2 only if nonzero.

analyses.keyLogicNotes: Trivial 23-byte body (0x42e1d4-0x42e1f7). The ONLY logic: `u8 v = *(u8*)(this+0x93dc); if (v != 0) *(u8*)(this+0x93dc) = 2; return 1;`. The write-2-on-nonzero is a "draw-tick acknowledgement" of the pause transition that OnUpdate initiated by writing 1 at 0x93dc. The TEST/JZ path (byte==0) skips the store entirely and falls straight to `xor eax,eax / inc eax / leave / ret` (returns 1). Cross-ref confirms OnDraw is registered as a DATA pointer at 0x42f428 inside RegisterChain (0x42f3c5), matching the th07 Chain callback table layout. No thresholds, no arrays, no bitfield math inside this function itself (those live in OnUpdate). The 0x1fa4/0x1b6c/0x120c/0x201b/0x1be3/0x1283 frame-count thresholds, 999999999 score cap, and 0x8d55e (577759) score-delta clamp are all in OnUpdate (0x42d8d5), NOT here.
analyses.reimHints: Define as `static int __fastcall OnDraw(GameManager* thisptr)` (ECX = this). MSVC 7.0 emits a standard frame: PUSH EBP / MOV EBP,ESP / PUSH ECX / MOV [EBP-4],ECX then reads ECX back into EAX twice. To reproduce byte-exact, write it as a non-inline function with frame pointer (do NOT use /O1-style tail optimization). No #pragma auto_inline needed (it's tiny but already standalone with its own RET at 0x42f7). Use byte access (u8/char) for +0x93dc, NOT int. The boolean `if (*(char*)(this+0x93dc) != '\\0')` must remain a single byte test; expanding to int compare would break objdiff. Return type is undefined4 in Ghidra but semantically int (1).

analyses.keyLogicNotes: Critical details for exact reimpl: (1) Ghidra's decompiler MISLABELS this function. It shows `FUN_0043a05f(param_1,param_1)` and a stream of `param_1`-derived calls, but the DISASSEMBLY reveals that after saving incoming ECX (GameManager*) into a stack local at [EBP-8], every subsequent CALL uses a HARDCODED ECX immediate (0x575950 Supervisor, 0x4ba0d8 SoundMgr, 0x1347938 COleDispParams, 0x134ce18 EffectMgr). The GameManager* is ONLY ever re-read at offset +0x93d8. So the C++ source is calls to OTHER singletons, not GameManager member calls. (2) The midi teardown block is guarded by TWO conditions: `byte[0x575a87] == 2` (Supervisor state == 2) AND `dword[0x575acc] != 0` (midi device open). Only then: MidiClose(0x575acc) -> MidiOpen(0x575acc, 30) -> MidiPlay(0x575acc). analyses.semantics: This is the per-frame Item/collectible update dispatcher, called every tick. It walks a fixed 1100-entry array of Item structs (0x288 bytes each) embedded in the GameManager starting at param_1+0xae2f0, counting live entries (flag byte +0x27d) into param_1+0xae2ec and maintaining a singly-linked active-list (head/tail pointers at +0xae574/+0xae578, next at item+0x284). For each live item it advances position by velocity, handles despawn when off-screen (funneling back through FUN_0042f526 which decrements player lives), then for collected items a 10-way switch on item type byte *(item+0x27c) (0=small power, 1=point/value, 2=large power, 3=star/pattern, 4=full power, 5=bomb, 6=life-piece, 7=point-with-graze, 8=score, 9=life-piece-alt) awards score, graze, life and bomb counters via the Score sub-struct at *(param_1+8) and calls the Supervisor (sound/bomb/life state) plus AnmManager number-popup helper FUN_004024f0. A progress/lives-pieces ladder at *(+0x8)+0x2c..0x30 uses tiered thresholds (0x4b/0x96/200 scaling) gated by difficulty DAT_00626280. The function ends early with a fatal assert (FUN_0044c930) if bVar3 (item collected-flag) was set on a previous iteration, i.e. it asserts single-pass collection.

analyses.keyLogicNotes:
LOOP STRUCTURE: do-while over local_8 in [0,1099], local_28 = param_1 (base, then += 0x288 each iter). The 1100-entry Item array starts at param_1+0xae2f0, NOT param_1+0. So local_28 is actually a misnomer — it iterates &item[i] = param_1 + 0xae2f0 + i*0x288, but the decompiler folded the base into param_1 because it rewrote `param_1 + 0xae2f0 + i*0x288` as `param_1` re-cast. Actually NO: the decompiler shows local_28=param_1 and increments by 0x288, meaning the Item array starts at param_1+0 and the struct offsets like +0x27d are direct. BUT the function also writes param_1+0xae2ec/+0xae578 — those are AFTER the 1100*0x288=0xbb280 byte array, at the END of the GameManager struct. So Item array is [param_1+0 .. param_1+0xbb280), and +0xae2ec/+0xae2f0/+0xae574/+0xae578 are managerial fields AFTER the array (consistent with a ~0x9700+? NO: 0xbb280 = 766848 bytes which exceeds 0x9700). REVISED: the Item array occupies the bulk of GameManager (offsets 0..0xbb280), and +0xae2ec..+0xae578 are trailing bookkeeping fields. This means param_1 passed here IS the array base, and +0xae2f0 is param_1+0xae2f0 = the linked-list/active-pool head (the 'first item slot' address used as sentinel).

MAGIC THRESHOLDS:
- 1099 (0x44b): max item index (loop bound, so 1100 slots, item IDs 0..1099)
- 0x288 (648): Item struct size in bytes
- 0x44c (1100): alternate loop bound used in FUN_00433b20 (mass-kill), confirming 1100 items
- 0x3b (59) / 0x3c (60): auto-collect lerp activation frames (case 0x27f==2)
- 0x80 (128): RNG threshold for 'full-power' pickup branch in cases 0 and 2
- 0x7f (127): secondary RNG check; if RNG >= 128 do alternate path
- 0x1e (30): max cap on DAT_0062f644 (consecutive pickup streak), used as index into DAT_0049ecf8 value table
- 50000 (0xC350): base point-item value, also 'hi-score delta' reference in case 1/7
- 0x3200 (12800): small-value popup color threshold in case 0
- 0x4b (75) / 0x96 (150) / 200 (0xC8) / 300 / 500 / 800: life-piece tier thresholds at *(+0x8)+0x30, scaled by +0x2c tier and difficulty DAT_00626280
- 0x43000000 (128.0f): written to *(+0x8)+0x7c on power-up
- 0xbf000000 (-0.5f): written to item+0x25c on certain despawn (case 0x27f==0 with DAT_004bfee0)
- 0xc00ccccd (-2.1f): min clamp for item+0x25c velocity Y
- 0x40400000 (3.0f): max clamp for item+0x25c velocity Y
- 0x41800000 (16.0f): local_18 initializer (unused/stack artifact)

STATE MACHINE on item+0x27f: 0=normal gravitating, 1=locked auto-collect (set when player enters collection zone via FUN_00442370 angle test and FUN_004325c0 seeds velocity), 2=lerping into auto-collect (enters when 0x3b < lifetime < 0x3c, exits at ==0x3c).

SWITCH(item+0x27c) cases: 0=small-power-item (PICHUUN), 1=point item (auto-collect value scaling), 2=large-power, 3=star/pattern (FUN_0042d612 adds to +0x68), 4=full-power (sets +0x7c=128, +100 score), 5=bomb despawn, 6=life-piece (value=(+0x18/0x28)*10+300, min 10, or 100 if DAT_004bfeb4!=0), 7=point-with-graze, 8=score-piece, 9=life-piece-alt (same value formula as 6).

BITFIELD OPS on DAT_0049fbf4 (a Supervisor/UI state byte):
- `& 0xffffffcf | 0x20` : set bits 5, clear bits 4-5
- `& 0xfffffcff | 0x200` : set bit 9, clear bits 8-9
- `& 0xfffffff3 | 8` : set bit 3, clear bits 2-3
- `& 0xfffffffc | 2` : set bit 1, clear bits 0-1

BITFIELD OPS on DAT_004b9e48+0xd6 (a UI flags ushort): `| 0x40` sets bit 6 (set when item fails on-screen test, i.e. about to despawn).

bVar3 SINGLE-PASS ASSERT: bVar3 starts false; becomes true only after a collection branch executes. The exit guard `if (bVar3) FUN_0044c930(0x15,0)` only fires at the very end after the loop completes all 1100 iterations — but since bVar3 is set inside the collection switch and the loop continues, the real check is: it asserts that at most ONE item triggered collection per call. If two did, the function would already have returned early from the first. Actually re-reading: bVar3 is set after the switch, and the loop continues to the next item; if a SECOND item is collected, the switch runs again but does NOT early-return. The final `if(bVar3) FUN_0044c930(0x15,0)` is a DEBUG ASSERT that fires after the loop if ANY collection happened — i.e. in debug builds this would trap, in release FUN_0044c930 may be a no-op logger. IMPORTANT for reimpl: must replicate this exact control flow.

OFFSCREEN DESPAWN: `if (_DAT_0062f870 + _DAT_00498a80 < *(float*)(item+0x250))` (player Y + threshold < item Y) → item+0x27d=0, FUN_0042f526(3) decrements lives. This is the 'item fell off bottom of screen' penalty.

AUTO-COLLECT TRIGGER (case 0x27f==1 setup): `if (DAT_004bfee0==1) { item+0x25c = -0.5f; item+0x27f=0; }` else compute angle = FUN_00442370(item+0x24c), call FUN_004325c0(angle, *(DAT_00575948+0x14)), set item+0x27f=1. So DAT_00575948+0x14 is the auto-collect speed scalar. DAT_00575948 is a foreign global (Supervisor/Player), NOT GameManager.

GRAZE/COLLECTION-LINE: DAT_00575948+0x20 is the player Y position (collection line). If item Y <= player Y, point value = 50000; else value = RNG*-100+50000 (case 1).

DIFFICULTY GLOBALS: DAT_00626280 = difficulty level (Lunatic etc), compared <3 and <4 in the life-piece tier ladder. DAT_0062f88c = current hi-score or graze counter (compared against DAT_0062f888 in case 7/9).
analyses.reimHints: CALLING CONVENTION: __fastcall, single arg param_1 in ECX = GameManager* (== &g_GameManager == 0x626270). The decompiler's `local_28 = param_1` is just Ghidra folding the array base; in C++ the body is `for (i=0; i<1100; i++) { Item* item = &this->items[i]; ... }` where `items` is at offset 0 of the struct (or the function receives a pointer to the items array). Given +0xae2ec/+0xae578 are accessed as param_1+0xae2ec, the Item array must START at param_1+0 and span 0xbb280 bytes, with managerial fields at +0xae2ec+. Verify: 0xae2ec > 0xbb280? No: 0xae2ec=713836, 0xbb280=766848. So 0xae2ec < 0xbb280, meaning the managerial fields are INSIDE the array range — impossible for a flat array. CONCLUSION: the array is NOT at offset 0. Instead param_1 here is the items[] array base pointer (a field somewhere in GameManager), and the writes at +0xae2ec/+0xae578 are into PADDING/managerial slots at the END of the items array (slot 1099 ends at 1100*0x288=0xbb280; +0xae2ec is within slot ~712). This is inconsistent — RE-EXAMINE: the loop bound is 1099, so last slot is &items[1099] = base + 1099*0x288 = base + 0xbb1f8. Fields at +0xae2ec are within slot 712, meaning they overlap live items. THEREFORE: param_1 is NOT the items base. The decompiler shows local_28=param_1 incremented by 0x288, but param_1 is the GameManager, and local_28 actually = param_1 + (some array offset that Ghidra constant-folded). Given the task context says Item array starts around +0xae2f0, the REAL iteration is local_28 = param_1 + 0xae2f0 + i*0x288, and managerial fields +0xae2ec/+0xae578 are the Item-manager header IMMEDIATELY BEFORE the array. The decompiler lost the +0xae2f0 base because it propagated it as a constant offset into all item field accesses (so item+0x27d is really param_1+0xae2f0+i*0x288+0x27d). For reimpl: define `Item items[1100]` at offset 0xae2f0, and ItemManager fields {i32 activeCount@-4, ptr listHead@+0xae574-0xae2f0, ptr listTail@+0xae578-0xae2f0} just before it. INLINING: Do NOT inline — function is 4305 bytes with a hot loop; MSVC will keep it as a standalone function. CALL SITE: likely invoked from OnUpdate (0x42d8d5). STACK FRAME: ~0xf8 bytes, no alloca. The local_10 variable is REUSED across switch cases as a scratch int (assigned in case 1, read in case 2) — preserve this aliasing or introduce distinct locals and risk differing stack layout. For objdiff fidelity, use a single shared `int local_10;` scratch. FLOATING POINT: comparisons use direct float compares against constants stored in .rdata (_DAT_00498a48, _DAT_00498a54=2*PI, _DAT_00498a80, _DAT_00498a8c, _DAT_00498bf0, _DAT_00498d44, _DAT_00498d48, _DAT_00498b24); these are 2*PI and angle constants — declare as `static const float`. The expression `_DAT_00498a54 / _DAT_00498a70` in FUN_0043e4e0 suggests a radius/velocity projection constant. PRAGMA: no special #pragma needed; just avoid /O2 over-inlining of the inner switch by keeping case bodies as-is.

Loops count times: accumulates *ptr into local sum AND does `(*(+0x8)+0xac) += *(+0x8)+0xbc` each iteration. Returns the byte sum. Read of DAT_00626278 inside the loop is a hidden this-relative access (the decompiler shows DAT_00626278 but in C++ it is `this->field8->ac` since param_1 is preserved across the call).

analyses.keyLogicNotes:
FOUR byte-range sums added together (CRITICAL to preserve exact ranges):
1. Region 1: start = *(+0x8)+0x34, length = 0x78 (=0xac-0x34, computed at runtime via SUB EDX,EAX: `((*(+0x8)+0xac) - (*(+0x8)+0x34))`). This length is DERIVED from pointer arithmetic, not a literal. The decompiler inlines this to `param_1,param_1` losing both args.
2. Region 2: start = *(+0x8)+0xb4, length = literal 0x14 (PUSH 0x14 / POP EDX).
3. Region 3: start = DAT_00626274 (=+0x4 sub-object), length = literal 0x38.
4. Region 4: start = global 0x575a68 (RNG scratch table, 0x38 bytes), length = literal 0x38.
The byte-summing helper FUN_0042d75a has a SIDE EFFECT: each iteration adds *(+0x8)+0xbc into *(+0x8)+0xac (an LCG-style seed advance) BEFORE the byte is read. So the total +=0xbc advances equals total_bytes_processed = (0x78 + 0x14 + 0x38 + 0x38) = 0xFC = 252 byte-iterations per call. This mutation of *(+0x8)+0xac MUST happen during the checksum loop, not before/after — it is interleaved.
Context from FUN_0042e3da (reseed): sets *(+0x8)+0xac = *(+0x8)+0x3c (seed copy) immediately BEFORE calling this function; stores return into *(+0x8)+0xb0; then DAT_0062f884 = result + *(+0x8)+0x98.
Context from OnUpdate (0x42d8d5): same pattern — sets *(+0x8)+0xac = *(+0x8)+0x3c, calls this function, then DAT_0062f884 = (float)result + (float)*((+0x8)+0x98). OnUpdate then iterates 7 ints at +0x34, 2 floats at +0x60, 2 floats at +0x54, 3 floats at +0x70, 2 floats at +0x80, 8 ints at +0x8c, 5 ints at +0xb4 and zeros DAT_0062f884 to _DAT_00498c80 if any are out of legal range (int: 0x198f..0x1a02f; float: _DAT_00498bac.._DAT_00498c7c). The 0x8c array (8 ints) is NOT directly summed by this function but is validated in OnUpdate — its checksum coverage comes via the +0xac seed-advance side-effect path.
Param_1 (ECX) is preserved across calls because the helper reaches DAT_00626278 via the GLOBAL pointer; in clean C++ this should be `this->m_subStruct->...`.
The 0x1fa4 / 0x1b6c / 0x120c / 0x8d55e / 999999999 / 0x201b / 0x1be3 / 0x1283 magic thresholds in OnUpdate are for separate rank/pause state-machines, NOT for this function.
analyses.reimHints: Calling-convention trap: the decompiler produced `FUN_0042d75a(param_1,param_1)` and 3x `FUN_0042d75a()` — COMPLETELY WRONG. The real per-call args are set in EDX (count) and ECX (ptr) via PUSH/POP and arithmetic. Reimplement as a helper that takes (const u8* ptr, int count) and preserves the LCG side-effect on *(+0x8)+0xac. Mark FUN_0042d75a as `__fastcall` and likely `#pragma auto_inline(off)` is NOT needed — it's only 0x60 bytes and called 4 times; MSVC 7.0 will NOT inline a function with a loop this size by default, so leaving it out-of-line matches the disassembly (separate CALL sites). Keep the side-effect ORDER exact: the `+=0xbc` into +0xac happens INSIDE the byte loop, once per byte, before reading the next byte. Region-1 length is computed via pointer subtraction ((+0xac) - (+0x34)) — write it as `(intptr_t)&sub->ac - (intptr_t)&sub->arr34` to reproduce the SUB EDX,EAX, OR hardcode 0x78 if struct layout guarantees it (it does, since these are contiguous). DAT_00626278 / DAT_00626274 / 0x575a68 are absolute globals in the original but should be expressed as this->m_subStruct (+0x8), this->m_subObject2 (+0x4), and an external RNG scratch table g_RngScratch[0x38] respectively, to match g_GameManager-relative C++ semantics.

analyses.semantics: Small leaf helper that reports whether the game is currently "active" (in a playable state). It reads the GameManager struct's sub-object pointer at +0x8 (DAT_00626278, the score/player sub-struct); if that pointer is NULL it returns 0 (not active). Otherwise it inspects a deep int32 status field at +0x1fbac inside the sub-struct and returns 1 only when that field is >= 0 OR exactly -2 (0xFFFFFFFE); any other negative value (i.e. the field is negative and not -2) returns 0. Callers (OnUpdate at 0x42d8d5, the bullet/enemy update at 0x420620, and several stage controllers) use the return to gate progression code-paths: when 0, the game is considered paused/menu/transitioning and many update side-effects are skipped. Despite its trivial body, Ghidra does NOT mark it as inlined (it has a real RET and is a standalone function body 0x42ad66-0x42adaa), and it is called from at least 8 sites.

fields.semantics: Pointer (GameManager->score/player sub-struct, aka DAT_00626278). Read to test for NULL and then dereferenced to reach the status field. NULL means not-active.

Game-state / activity-status code inside the sub-struct. Semantics: value >= 0 => active; value == -2 (0xFFFFFFFE) => also active (special "active" sentinel); any other negative => not active. Likely a stage-phase or game-mode enum where negative values encode menu/pause/transition/game-over states and -2 is an explicit 'still active during boss/demo' override.

analyses.keyLogicNotes:
The core decision is a two-level test that MUST be preserved bit-for-bit:
  (1) if (*(i32*)(this+0x8) == 0) return 0;        // sub-struct pointer NULL => not active
  (2) s = *(i32*)(*(this+0x8) + 0x1fbac);
      if (s < 0 && s != -2) return 0;               // negative-but-not-(-2) => not active
      else              return 1;
Note the asymmetric treatment of -2: it is the ONLY negative value treated as active. The CMP at 0x42ad90 uses literal -0x2 (0xFFFFFFFE). Equivalent C: `return sub != NULL && (sub->status_at_0x1fbac >= 0 || sub->status_at_0x1fbac == -2);`
Return type is int32 holding a boolean (0/1). The -2 sentinel is a magic constant that must appear literally in the reimplementation.
analyses.reimHints: Leaf function, no callees, trivial body (~17 instructions, 68 bytes). Do NOT inline it in the C++ source even though it is tiny: Ghidra shows it as a real standalone function with its own prologue/epilogue and it is referenced from 8+ call sites, so MSVC 7.0 must emit it as a non-inlined function to match byte-exact. Implement as `int GameManager::IsGameActive()` taking `this` in ECX (__fastcall thiscall equivalent). Keep the NULL-check short-circuit first and the `!= -2` comparison second so the generated CMP/JGE/JZ branch shape matches the disassembly (CMP [EAX+0x1fbac],0 / JGE / CMP ..., -2 / JZ). No #pragma auto_inline needed. No stack-frame quirks beyond a 2-dword local frame (local_c + compiler temp). Watch the calling convention: param comes via ECX (fastcall), NOT via stack/EDX, so in hand-written C++ declare it as a regular member function so MSVC emits __thiscall which passes this in ECX.

structFields.name: scratchBuf
structFields.semantics: Temporary malloc scratch buffer (AddedCallback allocs rand-sized, copies 0x38 bytes of init data, then frees). Repurposed each stage load. Do not confuse with vtable.

structFields.name: playerSub
structFields.semantics: Pointer to player/character sub-object (operator_new(0x38)=56 bytes). Field +0x1c=player-count/mode flag (2 if stage>3, 8 if extra); +0x25=bonus-extend gate flag. Also summed (0x38 bytes) by CalculateChecksum.

structFields.name: scoreSub
structFields.semantics: Pointer to score/stats sub-struct (operator_new(0xC8)=200 bytes). Primary scoring/rank/CRC structure; see subStructs. Written by AddedCallback, read everywhere.

structFields.name: flagC
structFields.semantics: Generic flag/state. Written 0 at AddedCallback success-tail.

structFields.name: difficulty
structFields.semantics: Difficulty/stage selector index (0-5). Drives extend/bomb threshold switch and 1<<difficulty bitmask at +0x14. Compared <4 for point-base 50 vs 200.

structFields.name: difficultyMask
structFields.semantics: = 1 << (difficulty & 0x1f). Difficulty bitmask.

structFields.name: rankForceFlag
structFields.semantics: Rank/continue 'force trigger' flag (DAT_0062627d). Written 0 or 1 by enemy-count tier block in OnUpdate; read by pause-guard (==0 required to allow pause). Cleared by DeletedCallback.

structFields.name: stageActiveGate
structFields.semantics: Checked at OnUpdate end: if nonzero, runs enemy-count rank tier block. Likely 'stage active / enemies present' or 'in boss' flag.

structFields.name: zeroInitRegion0
structFields.semantics: Large cleared region (FUN_00401a00 zeroes 0x93 dwords here, then more chunks at +0x14bc, +0x74e8, +0x8e34, +0xa09c). Exact field boundaries unknown; contains bullet/item/effect embedded tables accessed by other subsystems via their own globals (DAT_004bdad8 etc). Treat as opaque reserved.

structFields.name: pauseEngagedLatch
structFields.semantics: Set to 1 when pause is triggered (alongside +0x93dc). 'Pause engaged' latched flag.

structFields.name: flag93d4
structFields.semantics: Flag byte cleared to 0 during AddedCallback init.

structFields.name: val93d5
structFields.semantics: Multiplier read for computing +0x93d7 = +0x93d6 + 2*this.

structFields.name: val93d6
structFields.semantics: Addend read for computing +0x93d7.

structFields.name: packedSize93d7
structFields.semantics: = +0x93d6 + 2*+0x93d5 (packed size/stride calc).

structFields.name: statusBitfield
structFields.semantics: Status bitfield (DAT_0062f648). bit1=currently-paused (drives pause-frame accounting at +0x93e0). bit2=(+0x93dc==0 && +0x93dd==0)<<2 recomputed each frame. bit4 cleared (&=~0x10) on AddedCallback success. bit2 of THIS field cleared by DeletedCallback (& 0xfffffffb). Also holds global options: bit0=extra, bit2=show-fps, bit3=practice.

structFields.name: difficultySelector
structFields.semantics: Stage/difficulty selector (0/1/2) indexing pause-frame threshold pairs (0x1fa4/0x201b, 0x1b6c/0x1be3, 0x120c/0x1283).

structFields.name: pauseFrameCounter
structFields.semantics: Pause-watched-frame counter. Incremented each paused frame. First threshold -> Supervisor chain-swap + BGM fade; second threshold -> force quit (return 3).

structFields.name: randSeedWord
structFields.semantics: Written from DAT_0049fe20 (u16 random-seed word) during AddedCallback.

structFields.name: frameCounter
structFields.semantics: Per-frame-active counter. Zeroed by RegisterChain, incremented once per normal update tick in OnUpdate, reset to 0 by DeletedCallback. 'Frames since stage start'.

structFields.name: playCount
structFields.semantics: Stage play counter. Incremented by 1 at AddedCallback tail. Used to pick ECL script index, compared !=1 for seed, writes extend/bomb records (value-1).

structFields.name: anmColorSetup
structFields.semantics: 8 dwords at +0x95f4..0x9610 initialized by FUN_0042d657 from constant pool DAT_00498xxx (Anm/sprite color setup).

structFields.name: randSumFloat
structFields.semantics: Float derived from randSum+seed, populated by callee FUN_004012b0.

structFields.name: extendThresholdA
structFields.semantics: Primary extend/high-score threshold. Per-difficulty: base +0x88 + 200000/250000/300000/400000; extra-mode boosted +50000..+200000 by playCount.

structFields.name: extendThresholdB
structFields.semantics: Secondary extend threshold. = +0x88 (initial score) initially; stages 4-5 non-practice +200000/300000; extra mirrors +0x9618.

structFields.name: initScoreMirror
structFields.semantics: Mirror of score-struct +0x88 (initial score). Written = *(scoreSub+0x88) twice.

structFields.name: counter9628
structFields.semantics: Counter cleared to 0 during AddedCallback init.

structFields.name: rankCounter
structFields.semantics: Rank-up counter. Incremented in two OnUpdate blocks (DAT_00575ad8 tier, DAT_009a9a80 enemy tier). Reset 0 when enemy count <0x80. Used modulo 2/3/4/5/6 for skip gating. Drives difficulty/rank progression.

structFields.name: stageColorA
structFields.semantics: Loaded by FUN_0042e38c from DAT_0049f5d0 table indexed by stage DAT_00626280. Color/tone per stage.

structFields.name: stageColorB
structFields.semantics: Loaded by FUN_0042e38c; second stage color dword.

structFields.name: stageColorC
structFields.semantics: Loaded by FUN_0042e38c; third stage color dword.

structFields.name: counter9640
structFields.semantics: Counter cleared to 0 at AddedCallback tail. Subtracted-from (with borrow into +0x9634) by FUN_0042f526 (life decrement).

structFields.name: updateChainNode
structFields.semantics: Embedded UPDATE chain node (size 0x20). +0x0 priority(i16=2), +0x4 OnUpdate=FUN_0042d8d5, +0x8 Added=FUN_0042e83e, +0xc Deleted=FUN_0042f2e4, +0x10 prev, +0x14 next, +0x18 unused, +0x1c thisPtr=&g_GameManager. Inserted via FUN_0042fbd0.

structFields.name: drawChainNode
structFields.semantics: Embedded DRAW chain node (size 0x20). +0x0 priority(i16=2), +0x4 OnDraw=FUN_0042e1d4, +0x8 Added=NULL, +0xc Deleted=NULL, +0x10 prev, +0x14 next, +0x1c thisPtr=&g_GameManager. Inserted via FUN_0042fca0.

structFields.name: extendRecords
structFields.semantics: Per-stage/chara extend-lives-granted record block. Indexed by 0x8454 + stageIndex*0x1c + charaIndex*0x1c. Set to max(prev, playCount-1) when scoreSub+0x20==0. Companion bomb-record at +0x845a (same indexing). NOTE: this offset is INSIDE the opaque 0x24c region above; exact array bounds need the stage/chara max to resolve.

structFields.name: liveItemCount
structFields.semantics: Live item count this frame (OnItemUpdate). Reset to 0 at function start, incremented for every item with active flag (+0x27d)!=0.

structFields.name: items
structFields.semantics: Item array base. 1100 entries * 0x288 = 0xbb280 bytes. Iterated by OnItemUpdate with stride 0x288, loop guard <1099 (0x44b). Item struct fields: +0x24c pos[3], +0x258 vel[3], +0x264 autoCollectVel[3], +0x270 lifetimeSnapshot, +0x274 subFrameFrac, +0x278 lifetimeFrames, +0x27c type(u8), +0x27d alive(bool), +0x27f moveState(u8), +0x280 forceCollect(bool), +0x1e0 eclScriptHandle(ptr), +0x284 nextActive(ptr). NOTE: array base placement is CONTRADICTED by chain-node fields at +0x9644 (see contradictions) — resolve by confirming the true base offset.

structFields.name: activeItemListHead
structFields.semantics: Active-item singly-linked-list head pointer. Cleared to NULL at OnItemUpdate start; rebuilt as items pass on-screen test.

structFields.name: activeItemListTail
structFields.semantics: Active-item list tail pointer. Initialized to items base (+0xae2f0) at OnItemUpdate entry; updated as items appended (tail->next=item).

synthesis.structSizeGuess: 0x1695xx (if Item array base = +0xae2f0, last byte = 0xae2f0 + 1100*0x288 = 0x169570). The chain nodes at +0x9644/+0x9664 (+0x20) give a floor of >=0x9684. The Item array is the dominant region. The opaque cleared region (FUN_00401a00 zeroes through +0xa09c+0x1c3e*4=+0xbd2c) sits between the header fields and the Item array. CRITICAL: the relative placement of the Item array base (is it +0xae2f0 as the head/tail pointers imply, or a different base) is the single biggest open question — see contradictions.

methods.name: GameManager::RegisterChain
methods.semantics: Registers GameManager singleton into global Chain system: fills update node (+0x9644, OnUpdate/Added/Deleted=this+0x95e8=0) and draw node (+0x9664, OnDraw only); calls Chain::InsertNode(0x626218, node, prio=2) for each. Returns 0 on success, -1 if update-node insert fails (skips draw).

methods.name: GameManager::CutChain
methods.semantics: Detaches both chain nodes via Chain::Cut(0x626218, node) twice (update @0x62f8b4, draw @0x62f8d4), then clamps running score *(scoreSub+0x4) to <=999999999 (unsigned) and copies it into displayed *(scoreSub+0x0).

methods.name: GameManager::OnUpdate
methods.semantics: Per-frame Chain update callback. Pause detection (6-way && guard), anti-tamper RNG seeding (4 samples), rank state machine (DAT_00575ad8 + DAT_009a9a80 tier blocks, early-return 3), pause-frame accounting (+0x93e0 thresholds per difficulty), integrity CRC (FUN_0042d7be + 6 range-check loops), score smoothing (clamp 999999999, delta=(target-displayed)>>5 clamp 0x8d55e, high-score update), Supervisor vtable OnUpdate. Returns 1 normal tick / 3 skip.

methods.name: GameManager::OnDraw
methods.semantics: Trivial draw-tick acknowledgement: if +0x93dc != 0, sets it to 2. Always returns 1. No actual rendering.

methods.name: GameManager::AddedCallback
methods.semantics: Stage-load initializer. Allocates playerSub(0x38) + scoreSub(0xC8), seeds score struct (FUN_0042e3da random fill), sets per-difficulty extend/bomb/graze thresholds (+0x9618/+0x961c), inits AnmVm, registers ~7 subsystem chains (effect/enemy/bullet/item/boss/supervisor), loads stage BGM ('bgm/th07_13b.mid' for stage 6), drains sound preload queue. Returns 0 success / -1 on first subsystem failure.

methods.name: GameManager::DeletedCallback
methods.semantics: Chain teardown. Shuts MIDI device (if Supervisor state==2 && midi ptr!=0: close->open(30)->play), drains streaming-sound queue (busy-wait FUN_0044c9c0), runs singleton teardowns (Anm/Effect/Item/Boss release helpers), nulls Supervisor redraw flag, updates two time accumulators, clears statusBitfield bit2 (&0xfffffffb), resets EffectManager singleton, clears +0xd and +0x95e8. Returns 0.

methods.name: GameManager::OnItemUpdate
methods.semantics: Per-frame Item dispatcher. Walks 1100-entry Item array (stride 0x288), counts live items into +0xae2ec, maintains active singly-linked list (head +0xae574 / tail +0xae578). Per item: advance pos by vel, handle despawn (off-bottom -> FUN_0042f526 life-decrement), 10-way switch on item type (+0x27c) awarding score/graze/life/bomb. Fatal assert (FUN_0044c930 code 0x15) if collection happened (single-pass guard).

methods.name: GameManager::CalculateChecksum
methods.semantics: Anti-tamper byte-sum over 4 regions: scoreSub+0x34 (len 0x78), scoreSub+0xb4 (len 0x14), playerSub +0x4 (len 0x38), global 0x575a68 (len 0x38). Helper FUN_0042d75a has LCG side-effect: scoreSub+0xac += scoreSub+0xbc per byte (252 total advances). Result stored to scoreSub+0xb0; added to scoreSub+0x98 to produce integrity value DAT_0062f884.

methods.name: GameManager::IsGameActive
methods.semantics: Leaf liveness check. Returns 0 if scoreSub (+0x8) is NULL, else reads scoreSub+0x1fbac: returns 1 if >=0 OR ==-2 (magic active sentinel), else 0. Used by OnUpdate/bullet-enemy/stage-controllers to gate progression.

dependencies.purpose: Chain::InsertNode — UPDATE chain. __thiscall ECX=0x626218 (global Chain controller), node=&gm+0x9644, prio=2. Sorted linked-list insert on priority, invokes node AddedCallback.
Chain::InsertNode — DRAW chain. __thiscall ECX=0x626218, node=&gm+0x9664, prio=2. Same algorithm, traverses from this+0x20 (draw head).

SoundQueAdd — pushes (channel,sub,name) e.g. 'Pause' onto Supervisor sound queue at +0x73c (30-slot, stride 0x10c).
BGM control — registers track id into 5-slot array at Supervisor+0x620 with duration from table. Starts pause BGM fade.
Supervisor::OnPause / UpdateTimeAccumulatorB — accumulates real time (timeGetTime delta) into play-time globals; freezes wall-clock.
Supervisor::UpdateTimeAccumulatorA — primary play-time H:M:S:ms accumulator (globals 0x62f4ec..f8) via timeGetTime deltas.
BGM fade-out by float (3.0f). state==1 schedules SoundQue fade; state==2 calls FUN_00436c90.
Chain task builder — operator_new(0x30), switch(0..4) wiring callbacks, registers via FUN_0042fbd0(prio 0xf)/FUN_0042fca0(prio 0x11). Launches stage-transition/pause-exit effect.

BGM loader: (channel, ptr). midi-mode plays via MIDI; wav-mode rewrites .mid->.wav and SoundQueAdd. Loads stage BGM.
Sound stop/reset for given channel.

MidiOut::Play (this=0x575acc). Issues midi play.

NOTE: this offset (0x1fbac) exceeds the 0xC8 allocation — likely the sub-struct is LARGER than 0xC8, OR this is a DIFFERENT sub-struct reached via +0x8 (contradiction to resolve).
